description_long: This is a single traditional moccasin-style shoe, featuring intricate beadwork in geometric patterns across its surface. The upper part is made from a soft, brown leather or hide, with visible fur lining at the opening, suggesting warmth and comfort. The beadwork is predominantly blue and white, with accents of yellow, red, and green forming symmetrical motifs along the sides and toe. The shoe is closed with a lace made from the same leather material, tied at the front. The sole appears flat and flexible, typical of handmade moccasins, and the overall craftsmanship indicates cultural or artisanal origins. The shoe is in good structural condition, with no visible signs of heavy wear or damage.
description: A traditional moccasin-style shoe with blue and white geometric beadwork, brown leather upper, and fur lining, closed with a leather lace.
description_view_0: Side view showing the detailed blue and white beadwork, brown leather upper, and fur lining at the opening.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the symmetrical beadwork pattern on the toe and the central leather lace closure.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the continuous beadwork and the smooth, flat profile of the sole.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the brown leather heel and the fur lining inside the shoe opening.
